statement: Xiao 2017 is the primary DMPPT taxonomy source: it distinguishes centralized MPPT from distributed MPPT and subdivides DMPPT by MPPT granularity at string, module, submodule, and cell level.
follow_up_needed: Extract page-level citations if this becomes a formal report or deck.

statement: String-level DMPPT uses independent power processing per string; NotebookLM reports DC/DC converter or DC/AC inverter variants sharing a common DC or AC link.
follow_up_needed: Verify figure numbers and exact circuit variants in the PDF before topology scoring.

statement: Module-integrated parallel inverters are the source's AC-side/module-level DMPPT family; NotebookLM maps these to microinverters or AC modules.
follow_up_needed: Verify AC module protection, monitoring, and service constraints against vendor hardware.

statement: Module-integrated parallel converters are DC/DC module-level DMPPT connected to a common DC bus.
follow_up_needed: Check suitability against building DC bus voltage, isolation, and shutdown requirements.

statement: Module-integrated series converters are DC/DC module-level DMPPT and correspond commercially to DC power optimizers in series strings.
follow_up_needed: Validate single-point open-circuit behavior and bypass/shutdown mode for moving facade elements.

statement: Module-integrated differential power processors process mismatch power rather than full module power; NotebookLM flags wiring and fault complexity.
follow_up_needed: Confirm exact DPP variants and failure modes from the source before rejecting for iWin.

statement: Module-integrated series inverters are an AC-output module-level DMPPT family with series AC connection.
follow_up_needed: Needs closer circuit/protection review; not enough evidence for practical selection.

statement: Submodule-integrated series converters are DC/DC submodule DMPPT and require changes inside the laminated PV electrical layout.
follow_up_needed: Requires vendor laminate/segment access; likely vendor-data-gated for iWin.

statement: Submodule DPP variants are discussed as subIDPP/subIPDPP families with communication, wiring, and isolation implications.
follow_up_needed: Extract detailed circuit constraints before any architecture comparison.

statement: Cell-level DMPPT is discussed as on-chip/integrated power management with low-voltage and cost/complexity drawbacks.
follow_up_needed: Treat as research reference, not a near-term iWin architecture, unless product evidence appears.

statement: Xiao 2017 uses hybrid mainly for PV plus another generation source or for control-system wording; NotebookLM explicitly reported that the source does not use the phrase 'hybrid DMPPT'.
follow_up_needed: If hybrid DMPPT remains desired, define it as project terminology rather than source terminology.

locator: Chapter 2.2.4 Electrical design of a BIPV system
statement: Chivelet 2025 identifies BIPV electrical design as central under partial shading and non-uniform irradiance; local text corroborated central, string/multi-string, microinverter, and DC-DC optimizer options.
follow_up_needed: Extract page number and exact figure references for formal citation.

locator: Chapter 4 and Chapter 5 installation passages surfaced by local text search
statement: Chivelet 2025 connects BIPV BOS to central/string/microinverters and DC-DC optimizers, and recommends power optimizers or microinverters when shading is unavoidable.
follow_up_needed: Verify whether the recommendation is general BIPV guidance or specific facade/louver guidance.

statement: IEC 62548 provides the standards-side architecture hooks: PV system architectures, PCE with multiple DC inputs, strings with DC conditioning units, and series-parallel configuration.
follow_up_needed: Check national adoption/current edition before using as compliance authority.

statement: The standard includes a PV array example using PCE with multiple MPPT DC inputs and a PV string constructed using DC conditioning units.
follow_up_needed: Confirm applicable safety clauses for DCU shutdown and IEC 62109 references.

statement: Photovoltaic Systems Technology 2024 provides a current inverter taxonomy: central, module, string, and multi-string inverter topologies.
follow_up_needed: Use as secondary taxonomy; Xiao remains stronger for DMPPT detail.

statement: Photovoltaic Systems Technology 2024 covers static and dynamic PV array reconfiguration for mismatch and partial-shading mitigation.
follow_up_needed: Potentially useful as a non-MLPE comparator after hard-gate data are populated.

statement: Photovoltaic Systems Technology 2024 reviews MPPT/GMPPT algorithms under mismatch conditions.
follow_up_needed: Do not confuse algorithmic hybrid methods with hybrid DMPPT topology.

statement: The 2024 book uses hybrid for hybrid renewable energy systems and hybrid PV/PEM fuel-cell systems, not as a DMPPT topology category.
follow_up_needed: Skip unless the project scope expands to PV plus storage/fuel cell/wind/diesel.

statement: The Hebert MPPT source discusses DMPPT/topology-based approaches and notes MPPT may be applied in the DC-DC interface or DC-AC interface depending on architecture.
follow_up_needed: Extract exact table/section references if comparing residential vs utility relevance.

locator: Chapter 4; local text search for hybrid
statement: The Hebert source uses hybrid in multiple senses including hybrid systems, hybrid algorithm techniques, and hybrid cases; this is not enough evidence for a source-defined 'hybrid DMPPT' class.
follow_up_needed: Need exact chapter context if citing hybrid algorithm methods.

statement: Haeberlin 2012 covers module/string interconnection, partial shading, mismatch loss, and electrical integration of solar generators.
follow_up_needed: Use for physics and practical yield-loss grounding, not DMPPT taxonomy.

statement: Haeberlin 2012 covers MPPT charge controllers, grid-connected system layouts with central/string/module inverters, and static/dynamic MPPT tracking efficiency.
follow_up_needed: Useful for validation metrics after architecture shortlist is defined.

statement: Khatib/Elmenreich 2016 is useful for modeling DC-DC converters, DC-AC inverters, auxiliary sources, and MPPT controllers in MATLAB.
follow_up_needed: NotebookLM-derived only in this pass; run local PDF text extraction before formal use.

statement: Mayfield 2010 provides practical definitions of charge-controller MPPT, grid-direct inverter MPPT, multiple MPPT inputs, microinverters, string inverters, and central inverters.
follow_up_needed: Useful as a quick glossary, not as an advanced DMPPT source.

statement: The BIPV windows review is useful for optical/thermal/window context but does not provide detailed electrical power topology or DMPPT treatment.
follow_up_needed: Use only if facade-window performance context is needed.

statement: The GitHub prompt repository is not technical PV evidence and should be excluded from this electrical-topology source map.
follow_up_needed: None.

locator: Synthesis from NotebookLM plus local PDF text checks
statement: For iWin/BIPV architecture, the evidence base supports separating DC-side DMPPT, AC-side DMPPT, and hybrid-energy-system terminology; it does not support treating 'hybrid DMPPT' as a source-defined class without an explicit project definition.
follow_up_needed: Define project terms before generating a decision matrix.
